title: FIX-489: Approval Status — Pending Approvals Surfaced at Top of List
description: This tour walks through the changes introduced for ticket FIX-489 (Approval status). The core problem was that tickets waiting for peer approval were rendered inline with all other tickets, making them easy to overlook. The fix ensures they appear in a dedicated, prominent section at the top of the ticket list so every team member can see and act on them quickly — either approving or flagging as 'needs defense' to immediately remove them from the approval queue. The tour follows the agent run context files in chronological order, explaining the rationale and progression of the fix across two Helix agent runs.

steps.description: This is the context record for the first Helix agent run (run ID: cmpalyq60007rfd0udul5ahdh) targeting ticket FIX-489. It establishes the working branch (`helix/fix/FIX-489-approval-status`) and confirms the target repository is `helix-cli`. This is the entry point: the agent was instructed to fix the approval status display so that tickets awaiting peer approval no longer get buried in the general list.
steps.title: Overview: First Run Context

steps.description: The Description block defines the product requirement driving this change. Tickets in a 'waiting for peer approval' state must appear in a visually distinct section at the top of the ticket list — not interspersed with other tickets. Reviewers must be able to approve or mark a ticket as 'needs defense' with minimal friction. When flagged as 'needs defense', a ticket should immediately leave the approval queue, keeping the list clean and actionable.
steps.title: Problem Statement: Approval Visibility Requirement

steps.description: This file records the second agent run (run ID: cmpbln4ad005ggz0uh7j85duy) against the same ticket. It shares the same branch and repository context as the first run, confirming that both runs targeted the same fix. The second run was necessary because a git conflict caused the first push to fail on a follow-up sync step — the actual code changes had already landed successfully in the prior run.
steps.title: Second Run Context: Continuation After Conflict

steps.description: The Discussion thread captures the agent's completion report for the first run, including verification screenshots attached as proof of the UI change. These screenshots confirm that pending approval tickets are now rendered in a prominent top section of the list, satisfying the core requirement from the ticket description.
steps.title: Proof of Change: Verification Screenshots

steps.description: This discussion entry explains the failed second run: a git push rejection due to a remote branch conflict on `helix-global-client`. This was a housekeeping sync issue, not a regression in the actual feature code. The agent clarified that the meaningful code changes were already merged successfully and the failure did not affect the correctness of the fix.
steps.title: Incident Note: Git Conflict on Second Run

steps.description: This is the most recent and complete intraview walkthrough generated by the Helix agent for this ticket. It is a CodeTour-compatible JSON file that documents all steps of the fix in data-flow order. Its presence confirms that the agent completed a full documentation pass after the code changes landed, providing a durable audit trail for future reviewers examining why the approval status section was restructured.
steps.title: Summary: Final Intraview Walkthrough
